summary: All 10 sampled D-fail-closed rows (2 per ticker) are recoverable: the concurrent 10-Q/10-K's XBRL DocumentFiscalYearFocus/PeriodFocus matches the 8-K's EX-99.1 'Quarter X Fiscal YYYY' label exactly, and Goal 6f's G2_ALL_FY_DISAGREE — which advances the *prior* periodic's XBRL FY/Q by one quarter — produces the EX-99.1 label in every case. D fail-closes because period_to_fiscal()'s calendar math on the prior period_of_report computes a different FY than the XBRL DEI tag (year_of_end vs year_of_start naming drift on Feb-end fiscal years).

rows_checked.ex99_quote: Albertsons Companies, Inc. Reports Second Quarter Fiscal 2023 Results
rows_checked.d_fail_reason: rule_f_fail_closed_fy_disagreement: math period_to_fiscal(2023,6,17, fye=2, '10-Q') yields (2024,Q2) (year_of_start convention), prior XBRL says (2023,Q1) (year_of_end). FY disagreement (2024 vs 2023) triggers Rule F fail-closed since period 2023-06-17 is not calendar-shaped (mid-month boundary). G2_ALL_FY_DISAGREE bypasses the FY check and advances XBRL (2023,Q1) -> (2023,Q2).

rows_checked.ex99_quote: today reported results for the fourth quarter of fiscal 2023 and full year fiscal 2023
rows_checked.d_fail_reason: rule_g_fail_closed_fy_disagreement_calendar: prior 2023-12-02 is calendar-shaped, math says (2024,Q3) and XBRL says (2023,Q3) — FY disagreement triggers calendar Rule G fail-closed. G2_ALL_FY_DISAGREE bypasses FY check and advances XBRL (2023,Q3) -> (2023,Q4).

ACI.reasoning: ACI uses Feb-end FY with year_of_end naming. Both sampled rows have XBRL prior periodic that advances cleanly by 1 quarter to the EX-99.1 truth. D fails only because the calendar math uses year_of_start (off by +1 FY).

rows_checked.ex99_quote: Academy Sports + Outdoors Reports Second Quarter 2023 Results
rows_checked.d_fail_reason: rule_g_fail_closed_fy_disagreement_calendar: prior 2023-04-29 is calendar-shaped (Saturday near month-end), math (2024,Q1) vs XBRL (2023,Q1) -> FY disagreement -> fail-closed. G2_ALL_FY_DISAGREE: advance XBRL (2023,Q1) -> (2023,Q2).

rows_checked.ex99_quote: Academy Sports + Outdoors Reports Fourth Quarter and Fiscal 2023 Results
rows_checked.d_fail_reason: rule_g_fail_closed_fy_disagreement_calendar: prior 2023-10-28 calendar-shaped, math (2024,Q3) vs XBRL (2023,Q3) -> FY disagreement -> fail-closed. G2_ALL_FY_DISAGREE advance: (2023,Q3) -> (2023,Q4).

ASO.reasoning: ASO uses Feb-3-end (52/53-week, fye_month=1 in some PIT computations, fye_month=2 in fact sheet which keys off period-of-report month). Both sampled rows: prior XBRL advances by 1Q to EX-99.1 truth.

rows_checked.ex99_quote: Burlington Stores, Inc. Reports Second Quarter 2023 Earnings
rows_checked.d_fail_reason: rule_g_fail_closed_fy_disagreement_calendar: prior 2023-04-29 calendar-shaped, math (2024,Q1) vs XBRL (2023,Q1) -> FY disagreement -> fail-closed. G2_ALL_FY_DISAGREE advance: (2023,Q1) -> (2023,Q2).

rows_checked.ex99_quote: Burlington Stores, Inc. Reports Third Quarter 2023 Earnings
rows_checked.d_fail_reason: rule_g_fail_closed_fy_disagreement_calendar: prior 2023-07-29 calendar-shaped, math (2024,Q2) vs XBRL (2023,Q2) -> FY disagreement -> fail-closed. G2_ALL_FY_DISAGREE advance: (2023,Q2) -> (2023,Q3).

BURL.reasoning: BURL has identical structural pattern to ACI/ASO: Feb-end FY, year_of_end XBRL naming, year_of_start calendar math -> +1 FY drift -> Rule G fail-closed. Prior XBRL advance recovers cleanly.

rows_checked.d_fail_reason: rule_g_fail_closed_fy_disagreement_calendar: prior 2023-04-29 calendar-shaped, math (2024,Q1) vs XBRL (2023,Q1) -> FY disagreement -> fail-closed. G2_ALL_FY_DISAGREE advance: (2023,Q1) -> (2023,Q2).

rows_checked.ex99_quote: GameStop Reports Fourth Quarter and Fiscal Year 2023 Results
rows_checked.d_fail_reason: rule_g_fail_closed_fy_disagreement_calendar: prior 2023-10-28 calendar-shaped, math (2024,Q3) vs XBRL (2023,Q3) -> FY disagreement -> fail-closed. G2_ALL_FY_DISAGREE advance: (2023,Q3) -> (2023,Q4).

GME.reasoning: GME identical pattern: Feb-end FY, year_of_end XBRL naming. Prior XBRL advance recovers all sampled rows cleanly.

rows_checked.ex99_quote: ULTA BEAUTY ANNOUNCES SECOND QUARTER FISCAL 2023 RESULTS
rows_checked.d_fail_reason: rule_g_fail_closed_fy_disagreement_calendar: prior 2023-04-29 calendar-shaped, math (2024,Q1) vs XBRL (2023,Q1) -> FY disagreement -> fail-closed. G2_ALL_FY_DISAGREE advance: (2023,Q1) -> (2023,Q2).

rows_checked.ex99_quote: ULTA BEAUTY ANNOUNCES FOURTH QUARTER FISCAL 2023 RESULTS
rows_checked.d_fail_reason: rule_g_fail_closed_fy_disagreement_calendar: prior 2023-10-28 calendar-shaped, math (2024,Q3) vs XBRL (2023,Q3) -> FY disagreement -> fail-closed. G2_ALL_FY_DISAGREE advance: (2023,Q3) -> (2023,Q4).

ULTA.reasoning: ULTA identical pattern: Feb-end FY, year_of_end XBRL naming. Prior XBRL advance recovers all sampled rows cleanly.

structural_pattern_observed: All five issuers (ACI, ASO, BURL, GME, ULTA) are big-box retailers with February fiscal-year ends (52/53-week calendar with FYE on the Saturday closest to month-end Jan/early Feb). Their XBRL DEI tags use year_of_end naming: a 10-Q with period 2023-04-29 reports DocumentFiscalYearFocus='2023' (the FY that ends in Feb 2024), DocumentFiscalPeriodFocus='Q1'. The EX-99.1 press release matching that quarter literally says 'First Quarter Fiscal 2023'. However, the production resolver's calendar math in period_to_fiscal() applies year_of_start convention to the same period (2023-04-29 with FYE month=2 -> April is fiscal-month 2 of FY2024 if you start the year in March), yielding (2024,Q1). XBRL says (2023,Q1), math says (2024,Q1) -> FY disagreement (off by exactly +1) -> Candidate D fail-closes via rule_g_fail_closed_fy_disagreement_calendar (or rule_f for non-calendar-shaped Albertsons mid-week period boundaries). The G2_ALL_FY_DISAGREE recovery candidate is precisely the right tool: it bypasses the FY agreement requirement, takes the prior periodic's XBRL DEI directly (which uses the issuer's actual published fiscal-year naming), and advances by exactly one quarter (Q1->Q2, Q2->Q3, Q3->Q4, Q4 of FY_n -> Q1 of FY_n+1). For all 10 sampled rows the advance equals the EX-99.1 truth label. Note the concurrent companion 10-Q's XBRL also matches EX-99.1 directly (no advance needed), but the production resolver intentionally only uses the *prior* periodic visible at 8-K filing time (PIT-safe), so 'advance the prior' is the correct operation.
caveats: The fact-sheet field name 'companion' refers to the periodic that COVERS the same quarter as the 8-K (typically filed same-day or shortly after). The production resolver does NOT use this companion — it uses the *prior* periodic (filed strictly before the 8-K). I verified BOTH: (a) concurrent companion XBRL matches EX-99.1 directly (no advance), and (b) prior periodic XBRL advanced by 1Q matches EX-99.1. The G2_ALL_FY_DISAGREE candidate uses (b).

For Q4 8-Ks, the concurrent companion is a 10-K with DocumentFiscalPeriodFocus='FY' (annual). Advancing 'FY' is undefined in advance_label(), so the assignment's literal 'advance the companion' would not work for Q4 cases. The G2 candidate uses the *prior 10-Q* (Q3) which advances cleanly to Q4. I confirmed this in 4 of 10 sampled rows (ACI Q4, ASO Q4, GME Q4, ULTA Q4).

fye_month per fact_sheet is 2 (period_of_report month). pit_prior_features.csv shows fye_month=1 for ASO/BURL/GME/ULTA (their actual XBRL FYE is the Saturday closest to Jan 31, sometimes falling in January). This is a minor cosmetic discrepancy; the fiscal_math computation uses whichever value upstream supplies.

All 60 of the across-ticker 5x12=60 audit rows for these tickers (after Tier filtering) show recovered_by_any_goal6f_cand including 'G2_ALL_FY_DISAGREE -> CORRECT' per the AB_summary in fact_sheets/*.json. The 10 sampled rows are representative. ACI is the only one of the five with both rule_f (non-calendar-shaped period 2023-06-17, 2023-09-09 mid-week dates) and rule_g (calendar-shaped Q4 ends) fail-closure modes; the other four are pure rule_g.

The structural FY-naming-drift I describe is consistent with the 'fy_naming_drift' flag from prior_audit in the raw evidence files, confirming the audit team had already identified this pattern. My independent verification ratifies their classification.
